extraction.one_line_summary: ALAPHILIPPE Julian attacked on the final climb (Columbia) from breakaway, won by over a minute while VAN AVERMAET Greg shockingly defended yellow by finishing 4th from the break.
tactical_patterns: VAN AVERMAET conserved energy before bridging to breakaway at optimal moment after lake Annecy Cat 4, not initial selection.
SKY controlled peloton tempo but MOVISTAR's three captains created tactical confusion, diluting their offensive capacity on Columbia climb.
ALAPHILIPPE strengthened progressively through stage rather than early attack, matching pure climbers late when others faded on Columbia.

rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: positive: finished 4th over Columbia as non-climber, extended GC lead to 2+ minutes but went dangerously deep for stage 11
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: positive: solid at 3:21 down while SKY demonstrated strongest team, positioning preserved despite not taking aggressive stance
rider_signals.FUGLSANG Jakob: neutral: deliberately stayed on wheels to assess rivals rather than attack, confirming wait-and-see approach early in mountain stages
rider_signals.CONTADOR Alberto: negative: cracked badly on Columbia due to unrecovered cobblestone stage crash damage, exposed on first mountain test
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: SKY riding for him as co-leader reduced from 2+ minute deficit to Van Avermaet, stronger positioning than expected
